tcgdex.flavor_text: Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.
Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.
They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.
A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. The plant sprouts and grows with this Pokémon.

This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times.
Apparently even modern technology is incapable
of producing a perfectly restored specimen.
These Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.

final_summary.surface_characterization_notes: In this 10-row sample, the rows look like legacy incomplete canon parents with consistent linked set and tcgdex reference evidence, but without a directly stored printed-number field. Embedded external_id/provenance_ref strings exist, but using them as numbers would require parsing outside this audit.
final_summary.safest_next_phase_based_on_evidence_only: Continue read-only provenance auditing focused on whether any authoritative stored numeric printed-number field exists for the supported null-parent surface. Do not repair from embedded external_id or provenance_ref tokens without an explicit evidence rule.
